type: MICU Note — Transition to Comfort Measures
author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Emily Rubin (Palliative Care)
department: Medical Intensive Care Unit / Palliative Care
content:
MICU NOTE — TRANSITION TO COMFORT MEASURES — UPMC
Date: 5/20/2027

After further family discussion, the surrogate (sister) and the patient's children elected to transition to COMFORT MEASURES, given ~2.5 weeks of non-improvement, refractory shock on two pressors, persistent fevers without an identified source, and a MELD ~40.

Plan enacted: focus on comfort. Non-comfort-directed interventions are discontinued — including the antibiotics (vancomycin, meropenem) and the vasopressors — with attention to symptom control. Family at bedside.

(Note: discontinuation of antibiotics here was part of the comfort-measures transition, not a targeted diagnostic maneuver.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Lena Park, MD (MICU) and Dr. Emily Rubin, MD (Palliative Care). 5/20/2027.
